mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in <50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in <40% of triggered months or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods show no statistically significant outperformance versus baseline (p > 0.05) over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, a rebound in the index above its 6-month moving average signals supply-side recovery that reverses bearish sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns fail to materialize in >55% of crossover events or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while price indices remain subdued, real rates increase without triggering recession fears, compressing equity risk premiums and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, monthly nonfarm payrolls growth exceeding 250,000 signals overheating that forces monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of high-payroll months during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates acute supply-side weakness that immediately triggers short-term equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in <45% of triggered months or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.
